```json
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "By default, subtitles attached to <code class=\"inline-code\">USoundWave</code> asset userdata are automatically played and stopped using the time the audio starts and stops.  However, there is a use case for using the subtitle duration property. We added an enum to provide a method for users to choose which duration to use.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Multiple subtitles are now supported in the userdata asset.  Rather than using multiple assets for multiple subtitles associated with a given SFX, you now instead have all the subtitles in one asset.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "You can now enable or disable subtitles globally using <code class=\"inline-code\">UEngine::bSubtitlesEnabled</code>.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Audio Waveform Editor",
      "level": 2,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26129134,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Audio Waveform Editor",
      "image": {
        "id": 26129134,
        "file_name": "image78.gif",
        "file_size": 2723327,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T18:30:24.116+00:00",
        "height": 869,
        "width": 1596,
        "storage_key": "3273165c-57be-4949-85c9-2930e3be6345",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "3273165c-57be-4949-85c9-2930e3be6345",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Often sound designers need to go back and forth from UE to an external tool when working with audio assets. To help their workflow in Unreal Engine 5.7, we added the display of markers and loop regions from imported sound files to the <b>Audio Waveform Editor</b>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This includes additional quality of life improvements to expand the wave editor to be sufficiently featured so that sound designers can stay within UE for common audio editing tasks.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Format / Channel Agnostic Types (CAT)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Sound designers often need to send and receive multichannel audio data between specific MetaSound nodes of varying formats and channel counts. In UE 5.7, audio programmers can code custom nodes containing input and output references using <b>Channel Agnostic Types (CAT)</b>:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "The new audio-rate data type provides a way for audio buffers to support multiple channels in one pin type.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Nodes can now support multiple configurations for audio channel formats.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Reduces clutter and complexity in MetaSound Graphs.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Tech Audio Tools Sample",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Tech Audio’s experience and usage of editor utility can be extremely beneficial to the game audio community. This initiative is to help onboard new developers to UE Audio, make working at scale less daunting, and serve as a starting point for developers to fork from in building their own project-specific tooling.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A set of blueprint scripting utilities, tools, and graphs to help with working with Unreal Audio technology.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Tech Audio would like to build an exemplar collection of editor utilities and workflows to use internally and showcase externally.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "j1jA",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Platform

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2526,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2054,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Android Platform Support",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We improved workflows for developers building, packaging, and deploying Unreal Engine projects to Android devices.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Deploy on Android 15 with 16kb page support",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Skip manual SDK setup adjustments on macOS.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<code class=\"inline-code\">bDontBundleLibrariesInAPK</code> setting is now enabled by default, ensures libraries are kept external for improved package size and deployment flexibility. ",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Mobile Renderer",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Unreal Engine 5.7 brings improvements and new features to the <b>Mobile Renderer</b>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can now use the Multipass deferred rendering mode which enables contact shadows and inset shadows.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In addition, SSAO, SSR and DFAO are now scalability options, which you can toggle at runtime, not needing to be set as project options and re-packaging anymore.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Stereo Layer",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Stereo layer components are now visible when the X axis points toward the camera. To enable legacy behavior (visible when X points away from the camera) use the console variable:<code class=\"inline-code\"> XR``.StereoLayers.UseLegacyFacing true</code>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Emulated stereo layers now support depth and are OpenXR compliant in how they render. You can turn off the new rendering using the console variable: <code class=\"inline-code\">XR``.StereoLayers.RenderEmulatedLayerDepth false</code>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "OpenXR Application SpaceWarp",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Application SpaceWarp is Meta's developer-driven optimization technology that can unlock extra computation power for suitable content. In UE 5.7, we implemented support not only for Meta's <code class=\"inline-code\">XR_FB_space_warp</code> extension, but also for OpenXR runtimes that support \n<code class=\"inline-code\">XR_EXT_frame_synthesis</code>\n\n. This technology forces the application to render at half framerate, and synthesize every other frame with an updated head orientation - greatly reducing the load on the GPU.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Frame synthesis (and SpaceWarp) is controlled with <code class=\"inline-code\">xr.OpenXRFrameSynthesis</code>. You can enable it at runtime, but it requires <code class=\"inline-code\">r.Velocity.DirectlyRenderOpenXRMotionVectors=1</code>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "OpenXR User Presence",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We can now return events when a head mounted display is donned or doffed for OpenXR runtimes that support the OpenXR extension <code class=\"inline-code\">XR_EXT_user_presence</code>. While this has been exposed through the <b>VRNotificationsComponent</b> in the past, the new method is more accurate and behaves the same between runtimes.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "N5Ak",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Motion Design

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2524,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2055,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Motion Design Production Ready",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26130092,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Motion Design Production Ready",
      "image": {
        "id": 26130092,
        "file_name": "image100.gif",
        "file_size": 2990512,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T19:26:59.286+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "e81e8e66-3059-4985-8234-17e755934f71",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "e81e8e66-3059-4985-8234-17e755934f71",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "As customers begin to implement Motion Design into their core workflows, the Motion Design team will continue to survey customer feedback and focus on further performance optimization opportunities for the toolset.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Getting Motion Design to a Production Ready state required the following:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Harden packaging support.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Refactor Text3D to support rich text and masking capability.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Transition Logic stability and increased ease of use and setup.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Enhance Remote Control functionality by adding interface organizational tools and refactor general UX for accelerated setup time.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Upgrade Scene State based on client feedback.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Create communication between Scene State and Transition Logic.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Ensure Material Designer reliability.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Text3D — Rich Text",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26130110,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Text3D — Rich Text",
      "image": {
        "id": 26130110,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 331780,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T19:30:14.413+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "c3223087-c392-40fe-bd42-d7d30aa0fbb3",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "c3223087-c392-40fe-bd42-d7d30aa0fbb3",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Give your text layouts a dynamic edge with both pre-configured or dynamically customizable text. Create a Text3D style set using our new dedicated asset where you can create a consistent configuration you can use across your project on any Text3D actor.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Previously, this sort of layout would require several Text3D actors and an Auto-Follow modifier. In Unreal 5.7, these can be edited in-line, reducing the actor count and even providing a way for you to remote-control the individual text sections.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Supports assigning fonts, colors, sizing,",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Flexible styling tools adapt to any art direction.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Ideal for designs with dynamic UI.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Exposes each section of the text line to remote control for further customization. ",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "This is useful when you want to break apart something like \"Brand New Enriched Text\" into three separate controllers while retaining the style of each word.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Transition Logic Quality of Life",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26130260,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Transition Logic Quality of Life",
      "image": {
        "id": 26130260,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 112292,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T19:35:38.258+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "7d432986-6c5d-47bb-a4fa-1d33ba89cce6",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "7d432986-6c5d-47bb-a4fa-1d33ba89cce6",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Transition Logic</b> is critical to the broadcast workflow and, based on customer feedback, we reevaluated the steps required to create a level and set up Transition Logic. You can now accomplish all of the steps in the image above by creating a new level and making sure that the <b>Create Transition Logic Default Scene</b> option is enabled, as seen in the top left corner of the image.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Below are the setup steps that Transition Logic automates when you enable Create Transition Logic Default Scene:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Create pre-labeled In and Out sequences.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Create pre-labeled Layer Change sequences.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "You can add additional (and pre assigned) Layer Change sequences with new entries in the Sequencer options panel.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Pre-set the sequence settings on those sequences for immediate compatibility.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Create a default layer so that you can get to work immediately and test your design.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Add the Transition Logic Sublayer Modifier to a null actor in your Motion Design Outliner and pre-assign it to the generated In and Out sequences.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "All that is required of you beyond that is to actually animate your content using the generated In, Out, and Change sequences.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Remote Control UX",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26130578,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Remote Control UX",
      "image": {
        "id": 26130578,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 748715,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T19:41:16.130+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "4aa2614b-f35e-4752-85c6-7c5c412d3394",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "4aa2614b-f35e-4752-85c6-7c5c412d3394",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We added or improved several features that save you time while rigging your designs.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>New Controllers</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Category",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Add categories to separate your controllers into groups.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Single select or multi-select your controllers and drag them into the categories.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "String List",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Curate a named dropdown list that you can link to your conditional behaviors to enhance the end-user experience.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Previously, solving this would require entering integers where \"1 = Blue, 2= Red, 3= Yellow,\" now you can name the dropdown entries Red, Blue, Yellow.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Events",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Create a button that can play Motion Design sequences, trigger an On Modify property, or trigger a broadcast event.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Path Behavior</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Previously, each Path Behavior entry could only address a single actor. There is now a list that appears under the Actions panel where you can add as many actors as you want to effect, reducing the need for multiple Path Behaviors and making it easier to troubleshoot your setup.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Reordering the path list was previously not possible; now, you can use the left side drag handle to reorder as necessary.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "The Path Preview now informs you if the path that you're routing to is valid or not with a green checkmark.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UI Enhancement</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "We added a link icon to the left-side properties panel to show whether an exposed property is assigned to a controller.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Scene State",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26130622,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "",
      "image": {
        "id": 26130622,
        "file_name": "image75.gif",
        "file_size": 2921130,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T19:44:12.227+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "181a0970-ad86-41d6-a59e-fd9b6ec958e6",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "181a0970-ad86-41d6-a59e-fd9b6ec958e6",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Scene State</b> in UE 5.7 received several new features and UX improvements that greatly improve user control.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "State Machine Organization",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Reorder State Machines by dragging and dropping.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Add categories.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Added Setter Tasks as part of property references.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Datatypes include: Boolean, Float, Int, String, Text.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                },
                {
                  "type": "enhanced_list",
                  "style": "unordered",
                  "items": [
                    [
                      {
                        "type": "paragraph",
                        "content": "Each datatype has unique functionality.",
                        "settings": {
                          "is_hidden": false
                        }
                      }
                    ]
                  ],
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Property Functions",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Concatenate String",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Text to String",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Name to String",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Integer to String",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Float to String",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Spawn Actors and Spawn Actors to Tickers.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Data Link",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Data Link </b>received two much-requested updates:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Push Functionality, providing a method for constant detection of changing values from feeds to trigger updates.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Interaction with with Scene State to provide a method for scrolling tickers and other living elements.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "rBdZ",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Production Rendering Pipeline

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2531,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2056,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Material Parameter Collection Modifier",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26133532,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Material Parameter Collection Modifier",
      "image": {
        "id": 26133532,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 537108,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T14:54:04.574+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "ed6055ab-461f-48ba-ba4f-316ee9a104a0",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "ed6055ab-461f-48ba-ba4f-316ee9a104a0",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Material parameter collections</b> are powerful assets that can drive multiple properties on multiple materials at once. Creative looks and effects within a scene are often driven dynamically by material parameter collections.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Material Parameter Collection Modifier</b> provides a way for you to set and override parameter values on a per-render layer basis in <b>Movie Render Graph</b>. Using the same level and level sequence, you can render shots with different parameter values on each render layer. You can also make parameters into graph variables and expose them as overrides in both the <b>Quick Render</b> and the <b>Movie Render Queue</b> windows.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Burn In Per-Output Type",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26133535,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Burn In Per-Output Type",
      "image": {
        "id": 26133535,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 1161882,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T14:54:06.100+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "088892bb-64ac-44df-83f8-6b81863d01a8",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "088892bb-64ac-44df-83f8-6b81863d01a8",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The ability to render multiple output types in the same job means Movie Render Graph can provide both final and review media simultaneously. Typically, Burn In is desirable for some but not all of the output types in a job. To account for this, Movie Render Graph now sets <b>Burn In Per-Output Type</b> instead of by using a separate node in a branch. This makes it much simpler to, for example, have clean EXR renders but include Burn In on MP4s.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "File Naming Control",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26133530,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "File Naming Control",
      "image": {
        "id": 26133530,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 85528,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T14:54:04.231+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "5447ba21-8fe8-47c6-acec-8c2a49216299",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "5447ba21-8fe8-47c6-acec-8c2a49216299",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Strong naming conventions on render output are a foundational building block of efficient pipelines in all industries. With this in mind, Movie Render Graph offers much more robust <b>File Naming Control</b> to better support studios' specific naming requirements natively, without the need for additional pipeline scripting. Key improvements include:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Custom Naming Tokens</b> defined in the Cinematic Assembly tools are supported in all name fields.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "You can customize {renderer_name} (for example, \"Deferred\") and {renderer_sub_name} (for example, \"beauty\"), and conflicts are resolved per-branch rather than for the entire graph, offering more flexibility.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Burn In</b> no longer forces {renderer_name} on the beauty render when it is not composited.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Post Process Material</b> passes can use a separate naming convention from the beauty render if desired.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "EXR Metadata Per Render Layer",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26133534,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "EXR Metadata Per Render Layer",
      "image": {
        "id": 26133534,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 465477,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T14:54:05.075+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "befb0db0-740b-4615-b953-b16f27b80fa8",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "befb0db0-740b-4615-b953-b16f27b80fa8",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>EXR Metadata</b> now follows a convention that reliably separates key/value pairs on a per-render layer basis and avoids naming conflicts. Based on this convention, metadata from Unreal Engine renders can be queried directly in post-compositing applications such as Nuke without the need for additional pipeline tooling.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "LX48",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Content Pipeline

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2528,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2057,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Customize USD Asset Import with Interchange (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134134,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Customize USD Asset Import with Interchange (Experimental)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134134,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 33294,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T15:41:16.489+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "adeab5cc-c991-4d9c-a8ea-71329b503275",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "adeab5cc-c991-4d9c-a8ea-71329b503275",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "USD is a versatile scene description file format with a growing adoption across the 3D industry.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We added USD to the list of file formats supported by the Interchange framework in UE 5.5. Content Creators benefit from more control over the import process and are now able to add custom processing steps for asset import of USD files, similar to the Interchange capabilities already available with other formats such as FBX.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The support for USD through Interchange is considered Experimental and limited to import of assets. In UE 5.7 we added support for:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Groom",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Dome Lights (not the DomeLight_1 schema)",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Audio (UsdMediaSpatialAudio)",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Point Instance",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "FBX Level Import with Interchange",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134135,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "FBX Level Import with Interchange",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134135,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 151881,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T15:41:17.103+00:00",
        "height": 510,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "b1a46672-e749-4c82-92f3-d37700f88bd1",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "b1a46672-e749-4c82-92f3-d37700f88bd1",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In an effort to align the UI/UX for importing different file formats into the engine, the FBX level import process is available through the Interchange Framework. This is now the default path to import an FBX into a level. You can still fall back to the legacy behavior with the help of a CVar.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The main change comes from the use of the same import dialog as for import in the content folder. Legacy functionalities such as removing assets or actors from import, or using a different set of options to import, can now be performed through custom Interchange pipelines.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "EgmO",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Editor and UI Systems

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2520,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2058,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "AI Assistant (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134160,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "AI Assistant (Experimental)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134160,
        "file_name": "image17.gif",
        "file_size": 3141735,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:38:50.387+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "4ce4c1e5-8bab-4d2b-9802-bf4d69a9d4c5",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "4ce4c1e5-8bab-4d2b-9802-bf4d69a9d4c5",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>AI Assistant</b> gives you a faster way to get answers without leaving Unreal Engine. By sliding out a dedicated panel in the Editor, you can ask questions, generate code, or follow step-by-step guidance, all without switching to a browser.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You save time and stay in flow. Instead of breaking concentration to search online, you can get instant help, examples, and troubleshooting tips right inside the editor.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Offer step-by-step instructions for tasks.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Get guidance and tips directly in context.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Troubleshoot development topics with AI help.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Generate code examples to accelerate workflows.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Reference past exchanges with chat history.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Copy and paste answers into your project.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The AI Assistant is powered by the same specially-trained AI already available in UEFN and on the Developer Community—tailored to understand Unreal Engine and help you move faster.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Home Screen",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134159,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Unreal Engine Home Screen",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134159,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 844610,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:37:53.709+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "784b0f86-4a84-4eb2-afcf-4bc373e1e006",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "784b0f86-4a84-4eb2-afcf-4bc373e1e006",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Home Screen</b> helps you get oriented in Unreal Engine by providing a welcoming starting point. It highlights essential resources such as tutorials, documentation, and forums.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You get an easier, more guided onboarding experience. Instead of facing an empty editor, you have clear navigation to learning resources, news, and support, all in one place—helping you start projects with confidence and direction.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Access the latest news and updates.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Navigate directly to Getting Started resources.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Open tutorials and documentation.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Visit the forums and release notes.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Launch links externally in Chrome.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Turn off the Home Screen if you prefer not to see it every time.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The Home Screen serves as a one-stop onboarding hub, making Unreal Engine more approachable from the very first launch.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Orthographic Clipping Interactions",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134163,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Orthographic Clipping Interactions",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134163,
        "file_name": "image99.gif",
        "file_size": 3110605,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:42:05.880+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "2e5f454f-2fee-4882-ad48-d1635770e749",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "2e5f454f-2fee-4882-ad48-d1635770e749",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Orthographic viewports now provide more reliable and flexible control. In earlier versions, clipping planes were often unusable, making it difficult to adjust what was visible in the scene.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You gain precise control over what you see in orthographic views, making it easier to focus on exactly the geometry you need.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Adjust near and far clipping planes with greater accuracy.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use new visual controls directly in the viewport.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Customize visibility in orthographic views to match your workflow.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This improvement, fixed in UE 5.7, makes orthographic workflows more practical and predictable.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Improved Save and Delete Dialog Boxes",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134161,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Improved Save and Delete Dialog Boxes",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134161,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 101804,
        "content_type": "image/jpeg",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:41:40.657+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "fe818cc4-3a97-4455-819a-1c95218b7e23",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "fe818cc4-3a97-4455-819a-1c95218b7e23",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We improved the <b>Save </b>and <b>Delete </b>dialog boxes in Unreal Engine to make asset management smoother. Common actions now work as expected, reducing unnecessary clicks and interruptions to your workflow.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You spend less time wrestling with repetitive prompts and more time focusing on your project.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use the Enter key in the Asset Deletion popup (previously unavailable).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Delete multiple assets without repeatedly clicking Accept with the mouse.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Rely on more consistent and intuitive dialog box interactions.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "These improvements arrive in UE 5.7 and streamline everyday workflows by fixing long-standing pain points.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Improved Window Dragging Interactions",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134204,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Improved Window Dragging Interactions",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134204,
        "file_name": "image15.gif",
        "file_size": 1675367,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:54:51.153+00:00",
        "height": 446,
        "width": 1024,
        "storage_key": "610fc139-afb8-402f-ba35-d3641a897467",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "610fc139-afb8-402f-ba35-d3641a897467",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Dragging windows inside the editor is now more intuitive and less disruptive. In previous versions, clicking and dragging on any header bar would undock the entire Level Editor from fullscreen, often by accident.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You avoid frustrating misclicks and keep your workspace stable, so you can stay focused on building.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Drag windows and panels without undocking the full Level Editor.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Prevent accidental breaks in fullscreen workflows.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Enjoy more predictable and user-friendly window interactions.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This improvement, fixed in UE 5.7, addresses a common frustration and makes window management more reliable.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Improved Blueprint Editor Duplicate Interactions",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134206,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Improved Blueprint Editor Duplicate Interactions",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134206,
        "file_name": "image25.gif",
        "file_size": 2087615,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:56:37.277+00:00",
        "height": 445,
        "width": 1024,
        "storage_key": "940f5768-67c5-480f-b047-522f7f5c28a1",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "940f5768-67c5-480f-b047-522f7f5c28a1",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Duplicating items in the Blueprint Editor is now smoother and less disruptive. In previous versions, duplicating would clear your selection, forcing you to manually reselect items in the tree view.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You keep momentum while editing, avoiding extra clicks and rework when duplicating elements.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Duplicate items in the Blueprint Editor without losing selection.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Maintain active selections when working in the tree view.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Reduce repetitive steps when duplicating multiple items.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This improvement, fixed in UE 5.7, makes Blueprint editing more efficient and frustration-free.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Geometry Inspection Viewport Modes",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134208,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Geometry Inspection Viewport Modes",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134208,
        "file_name": "image7.gif",
        "file_size": 2650214,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:57:37.187+00:00",
        "height": 514,
        "width": 1183,
        "storage_key": "0f83f10e-23e4-4219-acb7-b82f24dac15d",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "0f83f10e-23e4-4219-acb7-b82f24dac15d",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The new <b>Model Inspection Viewport Modes</b> in Unreal Engine provide simplified, non-photorealistic visualization options. These modes prioritize geometric clarity over realism, removing lighting and shadows when inspecting mechanical or complex 3D models:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Clay</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Zebra</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Front/Back Face</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Random Color</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "User Asset Tags",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134209,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "User Asset Tags",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134209,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 152895,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:58:45.840+00:00",
        "height": 721,
        "width": 1155,
        "storage_key": "e7bcdf07-b951-4e43-ba6d-c5ffd4dc747f",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "e7bcdf07-b951-4e43-ba6d-c5ffd4dc747f",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>User Asset Tags</b> system provides a way to add arbitrary tags to your assets. It supports local favorites as well as project-wide tags, and you can extend it to suggest additional tags. These tags serve as the foundation for additional systems such as the <b>Tagged Asset Browser</b> used in Niagara. Select assets you want to tag, and use Ctrl + T or select <b>Manage Tags</b> in the context menu.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Tagged Asset Browser (Niagara)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26144105,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Tagged Asset Browser (Niagara) 1",
      "image": {
        "id": 26144105,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 351932,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-14T17:38:30.097+00:00",
        "height": 792,
        "width": 1408,
        "storage_key": "617aef02-fc21-4706-bb3b-b67a247bda5f",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "617aef02-fc21-4706-bb3b-b67a247bda5f",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26144106,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Tagged Asset Browser (Niagara) 2",
      "image": {
        "id": 26144106,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 354484,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-14T17:38:30.210+00:00",
        "height": 504,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "0d51dcd7-060b-495f-862c-04b55e6e8ce1",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "0d51dcd7-060b-495f-862c-04b55e6e8ce1",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "An update of the previous revamp of the <b>Niagara Asset Browser</b>. Now generalized, it lays the foundation for asset factories that want to adopt the same browser. You can extend it by creating a new <b>Tagged Asset Browser Configuration</b> asset, defining your own sections and listed tags. This way it becomes possible for your projects to have their own sections and tags, or swap out the main configuration asset entirely in the Project Settings under Niagara Editor.\n\n",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "For help upgrading existing Niagara systems and emitters to use the new Tagged Asset Browser Configuration asset, see <a data-document-id=\"4596094\" href=\"https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/upgrading-niagara-tags-in-unreal-engine\">Upgrading Niagara Tags</a>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "9knx",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Framework

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2529,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2059,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "State Tree: Rewind Debugger",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134446,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "State Tree: Rewind Debugger",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134446,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 615646,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:29.594+00:00",
        "height": 1439,
        "width": 1183,
        "storage_key": "212f9a88-54f3-41c4-9574-fa901043abe8",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "212f9a88-54f3-41c4-9574-fa901043abe8",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We’re introducing a new <b>Rewind Debugger track</b> for State Tree that brings an enhanced, owner- and instance-oriented debugging experience directly into the Rewind Debugger. This integration provides deeper context around when and how behavior logic is executed, all while aligned with gameplay and animation timelines.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The new track includes a dedicated Details panel with a compact tree view, making it easier to visualize and navigate complex logic setups, including nested subtrees and linked assets. This improves usability when working with larger or modular State Trees.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "While this integration marks a shift toward a more unified debugging workflow, the original State Tree debugger remains available as an option. You can continue using the legacy interface if you prefer it for some use cases.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "State Tree Runtime",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134447,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "State Tree Runtime",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134447,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 265831,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:30.471+00:00",
        "height": 733,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "ff962086-24a6-49a2-a196-cbd3751766ac",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "ff962086-24a6-49a2-a196-cbd3751766ac",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This update brings a range of runtime-level enhancements that improve the correctness and performance of State Tree-based logic.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Live Property Binding</b>: Nodes can now push value updates to bound outputs dynamically during execution using Output Properties, resolving issues with outdated or static data in tick workflows.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>ExecutionRuntimeData</b>: Nodes can now declare optional, long-lived data that persists for the life of the State Tree instance, useful for holding execution state across transitions or ticks without relying on static globals.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Memory Optimization for Static Bindings</b>: Delegate dispatchers, listeners, and property references are now stored outside of instance data, reducing per-node memory overhead.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We implemented the following features but did not enable them by default to avoid disrupting existing data and default behaviors. However, you can opt in to use them if desired.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Re-Enter State Behavior</b>: Added support for re-entering the same state and restarting tasks, providing you with a way to implement restarting logic more easily.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Instance Data Refactor</b>: Conditions and transitions now use scoped instanced data, preventing data leakage between sessions and making possible both temporary and persistent usage patterns.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Mass Debugger",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134449,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Mass Debugger",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134449,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 142939,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:30.603+00:00",
        "height": 660,
        "width": 1253,
        "storage_key": "c22d5186-0b55-44a8-95a7-129e963e084f",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "c22d5186-0b55-44a8-95a7-129e963e084f",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We’ve introduced a powerful new <b>entity search system</b> integrated directly into the Mass debugger, giving developers a new UI for crafting and executing MassEntity queries. The interface mirrors Mass's C++ query API 1:1, allowing for precise filtering of entities using the same criteria and requirements as hard-coded queries.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can now create, save, load, and share custom queries, and immediately view the results in the <b>Entities tab</b>. This improves how fast and efficiently developers inspect and validate Mass state at runtime.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Additionally, we substantially upgraded the <b>breakpoint system</b>, offering more control and visibility when debugging Mass processors and fragment writes.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Autogen NavLink Multiple Jump Config",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134452,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Autogen NavLink Multiple Jump Config",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134452,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 1321558,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:31.621+00:00",
        "height": 778,
        "width": 1214,
        "storage_key": "81a2caf5-4e5a-4303-a36f-4fe62b041b22",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "81a2caf5-4e5a-4303-a36f-4fe62b041b22",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We’ve expanded automatic navigation link generation to support <b>multiple jump configurations</b> per navmesh generation pass. Previously, you could only use a single jump configuration, which often required overly broad tolerances to handle diverse link types (for example. jump ups, downs, and across). This also meant that all generated links shared the same area class and link proxy class, limiting flexibility.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "With this update, you can now define multiple distinct configurations, each processed in its own validation pass. This enables more targeted and meaningful control. As an example, generating jump-downs and jump-overs separately, each with different distance thresholds, area types, or proxy behavior. It makes possible more nuanced movement setups while reducing overgeneration or unintended overlaps.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "To ensure backward compatibility, we migrated any existing single jump configuration as the first entry in the new multi-configuration setup.pState Tree Usability",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134448,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "State Tree Usability 1",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134448,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 36488,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:30.555+00:00",
        "height": 397,
        "width": 1048,
        "storage_key": "d22a1b1e-52e6-4eed-a01b-900f52f7c151",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "d22a1b1e-52e6-4eed-a01b-900f52f7c151",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134450,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "State Tree Usability 2",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134450,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 41130,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:30.574+00:00",
        "height": 402,
        "width": 874,
        "storage_key": "0567b2f8-7ffa-4bc5-9413-5c85460fcae8",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "0567b2f8-7ffa-4bc5-9413-5c85460fcae8",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We're continuing to enhance the State Tree editor experience with several quality-of-life upgrades aimed at improving clarity and usability:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "You can now <b>copy and paste tasks, conditions, and transitions</b> between states, even across different State Tree assets. This makes it much easier to reuse logic and maintain consistent behavior across large behaviors.",
            "settings": {
```
