```json
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "We've also added the ability to <b>copy and paste individual property bindings</b>. Bound and unbound properties are handled intelligently:",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Bound properties replace their bindings if compatible.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Unbound properties carry over values if a value is pasted, or carry over the binding if a compatible binding is pasted.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Pasting a value onto a bound property is disabled.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Pasting an incompatible type, or if the source data is inaccessible, will trigger a validation error.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "You can <b>hide properties within nodes and instance data based on schema</b>, reducing visual noise and improving focus.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Mass Runtime",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134451,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Mass Runtime",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134451,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 222329,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:31.293+00:00",
        "height": 192,
        "width": 464,
        "storage_key": "3dc031b5-39a0-4a3c-8023-2bb3a269983b",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "3dc031b5-39a0-4a3c-8023-2bb3a269983b",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This update includes a series of low-level optimizations and architectural refinements to the Mass framework, aimed at improving overall performance, memory efficiency, and system stability in real-time scenarios.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The most notable addition is <b>Processor Time-Slicing</b>, which means long-running processors can be split across multiple frames. This helps reduce performance spikes and enables better distribution of heavy workloads, particularly useful in large-scale or simulation-heavy environments.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Iris (Beta)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "With Iris moving out of Experimental and into Beta, we’ve focused on solidifying key APIs and improving usability, maintainability, and performance across the networking stack. This release includes several internal design changes and quality-of-life improvements for developers working directly with Iris replication systems.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Actor Factory Overrides</b>: Actors can now specify custom <code class=\"inline-code\">NetActorFactory</code> implementations using <code class=\"inline-code\">StartReplicationParams</code>, providing more flexibility in replication behavior at runtime. This enables advanced replication setups without relying on global overrides.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Cleaner API Boundaries</b>: We removed the Iris <code class=\"inline-code\">UReplicationBridge</code> base class to reduce virtual call overhead and simplify code navigation. Most Iris systems already depend on <code class=\"inline-code\">UObjectReplicationBridge</code> directly, so this change streamlines the inheritance model and avoids unnecessary indirection.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Improved Replication Lifecycle Naming</b>: We clarified the <code class=\"inline-code\">BeginReplication</code> API:",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "The non-virtual function is now <code class=\"inline-code\">StartActorReplication</code>, clearly indicating its purpose as the entry point to start replication.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "We renamed the virtual callback to <code class=\"inline-code\">OnBeginReplication</code>, separating lifecycle behavior from system calls.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Better Consistency Across Iris and Legacy Networking</b>: <code class=\"inline-code\">OnBeginReplication</code> and <code class=\"inline-code\">EndReplication</code> are now consistently called regardless of whether Iris is enabled, ensuring behavior parity and making the transition between networking systems more seamless.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Seamless Travel Support</b>: Iris now supports Unreal Engine’s seamless travel feature, ensuring that replicated actors and states are handled correctly across world transitions. This is a key requirement for shipping game features that rely on persistent player data or transitions between large streaming worlds.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<br>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "0NzW",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Media

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2530,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2060,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Media Viewer (Beta)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26135570,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Media Viewer (Beta)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26135570,
        "file_name": "media-viewer.gif",
        "file_size": 3136258,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T21:10:50.494+00:00",
        "height": 433,
        "width": 1000,
        "storage_key": "9765fd5f-d4ab-4f8c-ac27-6a42159ffa5d",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "9765fd5f-d4ab-4f8c-ac27-6a42159ffa5d",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Often animators need to align their animations against existing reference footage. In 5.6 we added the capability to dock media (images, media textures, video files, live viewports) next to where animation work is done. Two AB banks in either horizontal or vertical configurations help compare two sets of assets. Zoom and pan controls can be used to further align content. In UE 5.7, the <b>Media Viewer</b> plugin is now in Beta as it includes the following workflow and usability improvements:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Enhanced controls such as rotation and zoom controls using ALT + RMB.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Ability to bookmark and quickly recall asset configurations with hot keys.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "The lock button now also mirrors the playback controls and timeline while in dual Player mode.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "onaL",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Simulation and VFX

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2525,

  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Destruction",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138674,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Chaos Destruction",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138674,
        "file_name": "image21.gif",
        "file_size": 2745381,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T16:52:09.112+00:00",
        "height": 480,
        "width": 854,
        "storage_key": "1bfac456-0d5a-42c2-8259-c6722ec80730",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "1bfac456-0d5a-42c2-8259-c6722ec80730",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Chaos Destruction</b> system is a suite of tools designed to achieve cinematic-quality levels of destruction in real-time. In addition to its great-looking visuals, the system is optimized for performance, granting artists and designers more control over content creation and the fracturing process through an intuitive, non-linear workflow.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Reset State</b>: You can now reset the state of the geometry collection component using C++, Blueprint, or live in the editor while simulating.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Export to Skeletal Mesh</b>: You can now export geometry collection assets to a skeletal mesh asset.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Leaf Sub-trace</b>: After tracing against a geometry collection, you can now get the exact leaf cone from a line trace. When unbroken, the original line trace against the component returns the root bone. With this new function, you can now get the exact bone the trace is hitting. This makes possible things like setting decals on the right bones or even anchoring individual pieces at runtime. ",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Dataflow Editor",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_X7q1YU",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": null,
      "video": {
        "height": 2160,
        "width": 3840,
        "identifier": "V_X7q1YU"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Dataflow </b>graph makes possible the procedural generation of assets within a node-based environment. The implementation of the graph uses a pull-based evaluation, where the nodes of the graph generate a cached output state based on the evaluation of its inputs. Dataflow assets are used to assist in the procedural authoring and setup of simulation systems.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Chaos Cloth, Destruction, Flesh, and Hair use the unified Dataflow node-based toolset.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "New timeline and simulation controls.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A new toolbar.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A redesigned Paint Weight Map tool (to match the Skeletal Mesh Editor).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A new Edit Skin Weight tool (to match the Skeletal Mesh Editor).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A new Edit Skeleton Bones tool.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Many quality of life Improvements.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Visual Debugger",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138677,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Chaos Visual Debugger",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138677,
        "file_name": "chaos-visual-debugger.gif",
        "file_size": 9162236,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T16:58:19.091+00:00",
        "height": 506,
        "width": 900,
        "storage_key": "8ea085fd-d82e-45cf-947c-a80b39b6a91c",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "8ea085fd-d82e-45cf-947c-a80b39b6a91c",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Chaos Visual Debugger (CVD)</b> is a versatile tool designed to record and play back the state of a physics simulation during gameplay. It supports recording of all active Rigid and RBAN solvers, including particle states and collision data. CVD is accessible as an editor tool in Unreal Engine and offers extensive customization options, such as choosing specific data channels to record, either through the UI or using console commands.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The tool also features live debugging capabilities, giving you real-time inspection of physics data during simulation. Additionally, CVD includes a variety of visualization options, such as particle state, collision data, and scene queries, to assist you in analyzing and troubleshooting physics simulations effectively.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Object Name Improvements</b> : In this release, we introduced a new feature called <b>particle metadata</b>.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "This new metadata replaces the old long \"Debug Name\" string.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "CVD now shows the name of the actor owning the physics body name, and breaks it down in different fields (Level, Component, and Bone index).",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "In addition, we introduced a <b>Find in Content Browser</b> button that leverages this new metadata and provides a way for you to find the Blueprint a specific body comes from in a single click.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Performance improvements</b>: In this release, we continued working on improving CVD's performance.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "CVD files with a large amount of data (specifically over 2 GB of collision geometry data) now loads up to 30% faster.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Reduced a stall of +10 seconds that could happen while loading the first frame of complex scenes (+93.000 objects),to ~3 seconds.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "This release also includes bug fixes and QoL improvements.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Cloth",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_aIPXwI",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": null,
      "video": {
        "height": 600,
        "width": 1107,
        "identifier": "V_aIPXwI"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "As cloth simulation and complex clothing become more widely requested in-engine, our legacy skeletal mesh cloth editor and workflow cannot easily adjust to future upgrades necessary to support those needs. Fashion and most character work has almost exclusively pivoted to using DCCs which specialize in panel-based clothing construction. In order to grow with these external DCC's we continue to develop our cloth editor in order to ingest and edit panel setups while refining the cloth setup workflow.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Chaos Cloth for Unreal Engine 5.7 includes the continued development of a Beta Cloth Editor. ",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Skeletal mesh clothing data integration:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "You can now add cloth assets and outfit assets as skeletal mesh clothing data.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "The skeletal mesh render sections can then be deformed by the cloth simulation setup inside these assets dataflow graph, removing the need for an additional cloth component.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>New reference space options:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Use the new ReferenceBone dataflow node to explicitly set the ReferenceBone rather than use the auto-computed one.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Use the new Local Damping space option to solve in ReferenceBone space instead of using center of mass.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Optimizations</b>: We implemented optimizations to the cloth game thread and interactor tick.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Accessory meshes (Experimental):</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "We added additional accessory meshes and updated the backstop constraint to be able to use this accessory mesh.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Currently, you can create accessory meshes by converting an existing sim mesh collection into an accessory mesh. If Imported Vertex IDs exist on both the source and target collections, you can use these to match up vertex indices that were reordered as part of a SKM import.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Core",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138678,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Chaos Core",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138678,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 166640,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T17:54:00.537+00:00",
        "height": 1600,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "04f0626c-e927-4cf5-9e9c-12f98b7ea326",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "04f0626c-e927-4cf5-9e9c-12f98b7ea326",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Chaos Physics</b> is continually undergoing updates and optimizations in order to give artists, developers, and programmers better simulation results. Rigid Body dynamics is the core technology that most game physics rely upon. As we make progress towards bigger and bigger physics-enabled and physics-based worlds, we need to make sure this core system scales and can handle those use cases. Like rigid body dynamics, queries are a core technology that is relied upon by a wide range of systems inside Unreal Engine, and like rigid body dynamics, we need to make sure that query performance scales to very large dynamic worlds.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Simulation performance improvements:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "More parallel simulation stages.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "We added the Experimental Partial Sleeping feature for improved performance of large unstructured piles.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Query performance improvements:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Improvements to sphere and capsule narrow-phase.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Improvements to the general query layer transform and scale handling.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Chaos Visual Debugger (CVD):</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Improved loading speed for scenes with large actor counts and complex geometry.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "QoL changes and runtime-debugger consistency improvements.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Dataflow physics assets:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Initial support for the creation of physics assets using dataflow graphs.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Many QoL changes and fixes across the simulation featureset.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Caching",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138680,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Chaos Caching",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138680,
        "file_name": "image14.gif",
        "file_size": 2217606,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T18:01:44.174+00:00",
        "height": 480,
        "width": 854,
        "storage_key": "103174f9-ca16-4c44-923e-3daae4d6f323",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "103174f9-ca16-4c44-923e-3daae4d6f323",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In Unreal Engine, <b>Chaos Cache</b> is a system within the Chaos Destruction framework that records and reuses complex destruction simulations, significantly improving performance and enabling consistent, predictable results for features like short films. You manage caching using a <b>Chaos Cache Manager</b>, which records physics data into a <b>Chaos Cache Collection</b> asset.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This cached data can then be played back at runtime to display the destruction without recalculating the entire simulation, making complex scenes more efficient and less demanding on hardware.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Geometry Collection Root Proxy Mesh Support:</b> Chaos cache now properly supports root proxy meshes and will switch to use them depending on the state of destruction of the geometry collection asset being cached and replayed.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Automatic Increment of Cache Assets:</b> Chaos cache manager now has an option to automatically create a new asset each time it starts a new recording session.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Session Start/Stop Controls:</b> You can now control when the caching session starts and stops. By default, the cache session starts when the Begin Play event is triggered for the cache actor (usually when starting PIE or Simulation). An option to disable this behaviour is available, requiring you to trigger the start of the session using the start and stop Blueprint functions.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Observed Component Functions:</b> Blueprint functions are now available to add, update, remove, or clear observed components of a specific ChaosActorManager.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Major Bug Fixes:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "When recording a cached Geometry Collection twice during the same session, events now always record properly the second time.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "When replaying a cache in Play Mode, the Niagara Data Interface for Chaos Destruction now always triggers trailing events during the replay as intended.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Hair",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_xnJmI5",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": null,
      "video": {
        "height": 584,
        "width": 1080,
        "identifier": "V_xnJmI5"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Hair and Groom</b> initiative focuses on building a robust simulation pipeline for realistic hair behavior using Dataflow, skeletal meshes, and guide splines. The system allows for animation-driven simulations, blending between kinematic and simulated strands, and advanced authoring features like guide deformers and painting tools.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Groom Asset procedural workflows benefit from the UE 5.7 update to the general Dataflow Editor improvement. We improved the corresponding nodes to provide for a more flexible setup:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Selection-aware nodes:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Most nodes do take a selection, which results in a more controlled and targeted setup.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "You can use existing selection nodes for other assets (Destruction, Flesh) to create selection sets and manipulate them procedurally.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Performance:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Groom assets with complex geometry generate the geometry for the construction viewport faster, and now cache the information to avoid recomputing it.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "This applies to other collection-based nodes across Dataflow Editors.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Simulate hair with guide splines driven by animation and physics.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Blend between animated and simulated states for realism and control.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Support for painting attributes, skin weighting, and guide swapping.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Fluids",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138682,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Chaos Fluids",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138682,
        "file_name": "chaos-fluids.gif",
        "file_size": 2967015,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T18:06:36.761+00:00",
        "height": 283,
        "width": 590,
        "storage_key": "eb9bee35-2ff6-4831-9735-2f16dfabb925",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "eb9bee35-2ff6-4831-9735-2f16dfabb925",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Fluids</b> project continues to expand Unreal Engine’s water simulation capabilities, with targeted efforts in UE 5.7 aimed at the Advanced Water plugin, mainly improvements for baked river simulations.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include: </b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Now supports virtual textures.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Improved surface quality (less spikes artifacts).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Various bug fixes and performance improvements.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "dedv",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Virtual Production

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2523,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2062,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Live Link — Data Preview",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138734,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Live Link Data Preview",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138734,
        "file_name": "live-link-data-preview.gif",
        "file_size": 2752157,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T20:15:32.382+00:00",
        "height": 464,
        "width": 900,
        "storage_key": "61e274a4-cbd1-4335-b173-d7fbe808c854",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "61e274a4-cbd1-4335-b173-d7fbe808c854",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can now preview Live Link data in the editor viewport without needing to prepare any actors or skeletal meshes. The <b>Live Link Data Preview</b> actor visualizes all your Animation, Transform, Camera, and Marker role data in one convenient actor.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We also provided actor components for custom editor utility tooling. <b>Mocap Manager</b> uses these to visualize your Live Link data.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Live Link Hub — Robust Recording",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138733,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Live Link Hub Robust Recording",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138733,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 1533234,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T20:14:32.034+00:00",
        "height": 761,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "6096ec0e-933c-4942-9afb-8d52f197911c",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "6096ec0e-933c-4942-9afb-8d52f197911c",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We improved recording robustness in Live Link Hub to cope with subject data changing size over time — for example when losing a subject's tracking then recovering it during a recording.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can upgrade recordings from previous versions to the new format using a right-click menu option in the recording list.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Live Link — Broadcast Component",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138743,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Live Link Broadcast Component",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138743,
        "file_name": "live-link-broadcast-component.gif",
        "file_size": 2849893,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:33:21.707+00:00",
        "height": 600,
        "width": 1304,
        "storage_key": "64897646-c86a-4d8b-8784-ff8783b9daef",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "64897646-c86a-4d8b-8784-ff8783b9daef",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can add the new <b>Live Link Broadcast</b> <b>component </b>to actors in your level and turn them into Live Link subjects for other machines on the local network to receive animation from. The Broadcast component supports the Transform, Camera, and Animation roles.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This new component enables workflows where you can use a lightweight Unreal Engine project for skeletal retargeting, then you can broadcast the resulting retarget pose to other Unreal Editor projects on the network, making UE the retarget DCC software. The framerate of the broadcast animation is equal to the game thread framerate.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Live Link — Pauseable Data Stream",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138745,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Live Link Pauseable Data Stream",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138745,
        "file_name": "image65.gif",
        "file_size": 2581317,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:35:48.188+00:00",
        "height": 610,
        "width": 1101,
        "storage_key": "8cebc6c8-cb01-4383-a0d1-860286a36735",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "8cebc6c8-cb01-4383-a0d1-860286a36735",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can now pause and unpause data from Live Link subjects in the Live Link panel and in Live Link Hub. You can pause subjects individually or in groups.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "There are also Blueprint scripting functions to control pausing from your custom editor tooling. Mocap Manager now uses this API to control pausing of subjects.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Live Link Hub — Unreal Editor Device",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138746,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Live Link Hub Unreal Editor Device",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138746,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 129103,
        "content_type": "image/jpeg",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:37:09.554+00:00",
        "height": 602,
        "width": 1000,
        "storage_key": "c819bc70-598a-4992-8611-86641306bcb7",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "c819bc70-598a-4992-8611-86641306bcb7",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In future releases, the recording device functionality of Switchboard will be migrated to Live Link Hub for multi-user motion capture setups. For UE 5.7, in Live Link Hub you can now control the slate and record stop-start of Take Recorder on connected Unreal Editor clients, using a new plugin called <b>Live Link Unreal Device</b>, which is experimental.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This new approach improves robustness of multi-client Take Recording, replacing the legacy OSC message system used by Switchboard, and provides user-controlled authority over what each client should record and which clients should be allowed to record.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Mocap Manager — Dynamic Prop Attachment",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138747,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Mocap Manager Dynamic Prop Attachment",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138747,
        "file_name": "mocap-manager-dynamic-prop.gif",
        "file_size": 2806871,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:44:27.567+00:00",
        "height": 547,
        "width": 1261,
        "storage_key": "3a6fdb59-c9d3-40ab-a785-37b7870241a1",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "3a6fdb59-c9d3-40ab-a785-37b7870241a1",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "When retargeting mocap there are often offsets between a performer's hands and the hands of your mocap character. This presents a problem when attempting to align tracked props with your character.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The new <b>Dynamic prop attachment</b> component provides a way for you to drive a prop with motion capture data but add a dynamic offset to keep it in your character's hands.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>PCapProp</b> component now has a Blueprint-overridable function to let users write their own dynamic attachment logic. An example implementation is provided in Mocap Manager that should work for a majority of single handed props.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Mocap Manager — Improved Performer Mesh Creation",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138748,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Mocap Manager Improved Performer Mesh Creation",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138748,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 441986,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:45:30.150+00:00",
        "height": 545,
        "width": 1140,
        "storage_key": "e4038d1c-81f1-4ca3-ad49-c23574750c98",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "e4038d1c-81f1-4ca3-ad49-c23574750c98",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "For the most accurate retarget result a skeletal mesh scaled to the proportions of the performer's Live Link data is considered best-practice.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We improved the workflow in Mocap Manager for creating this asset to work with stages where the stage origin may not be conveniently placed and for systems that use right-handed coordinate systems.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Composure",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138749,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Composure",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138749,
        "file_name": "composure.gif",
        "file_size": 3069575,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:47:06.272+00:00",
        "height": 555,
        "width": 1280,
        "storage_key": "13655506-c53e-4975-a882-ef5767f8eb0d",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "13655506-c53e-4975-a882-ef5767f8eb0d",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Unreal Engine 5.7 reintroduces <b>Composure </b>as a modern system for real-time compositing with both live video input as well as file/image media plates. The new Composure is a layer-based tool with numerous improvements over the legacy system, including:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A redesigned <b>User Experience</b> and streamlined workflow.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Performant <b>Shadow </b>and <b>Reflection </b>integration for live-action footage using <b>Custom Render Passes</b>.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "An improved <b>Keyer</b>.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Media Profile",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138751,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Media Profile",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138751,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 591487,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:48:11.104+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "0b4ee201-36ab-4e7d-915a-f1d968a7177a",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "0b4ee201-36ab-4e7d-915a-f1d968a7177a",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Historically, the management of live video input and output in Unreal Engine required multiple assets (Media Source, Media Player, Media Texture, and so on) to get from point A to point B. The <b>Media Profile</b> seeks to consolidate these into a single configuration asset for a given machine setup and provide numerous workflow enhancements, such as:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Dynamically add multiple Media Sources and Media Outputs and select the desired hardware option (for example, AJA, Blackmagic, NDI).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Simultaneously preview multiple IO feeds.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Capture Media Output.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Configure Timecode and Genlock with immediate feedback.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Conveniently access video IO functionality in related tools such as Composure with a single click.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Take Recorder — Hitch Protection (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138750,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Take Recorder Hitch Protection (Experimental)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138750,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 138816,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:48:10.846+00:00",
        "height": 466,
        "width": 1300,
        "storage_key": "88ee631c-ee71-4a4f-beb0-90bd77005c69",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "88ee631c-ee71-4a4f-beb0-90bd77005c69",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The UE 5.7 update adds support for <b>hitch protection</b> during take recording. You can now configure Take Recorder to dilate the recording speed to protect against data loss. If a frame would otherwise be dropped, Take Recorder can adjust the engine evaluation to allow time for the data to be recorded before catching up.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "More reliably record complete data.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Visualize areas of concern where data integrity might be lost.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Make informed decisions in the moment about the quality of your recordings.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Naming Tokens — Editable Text Widget",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138753,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Naming Tokens Editable Text Widget",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138753,
        "file_name": "image87.gif",
        "file_size": 21142,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:51:53.545+00:00",
        "height": 236,
        "width": 642,
        "storage_key": "c61648cc-ace8-4f78-afd9-b3b631fbdd90",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "c61648cc-ace8-4f78-afd9-b3b631fbdd90",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We added a new slate and UMG <b>Naming Tokens Editable Text</b> widget with direct support for naming tokens. You can now easily build pipeline tools with naming tokens support. When focused, the text box will display the unevaluated {token} string. When committed, the text box will show the evaluated result text.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Search and autocomplete {tokens} directly from the text box",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Immediately see the evaluated token value directly in the text box",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Available with the <b>Naming Tokens</b> plugin.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Timeline Templates for Cinematic Assembly Schemas",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138754,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Timeline Templates for Cinematic Assembly Schemas",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138754,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 128287,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:52:34.067+00:00",
        "height": 624,
        "width": 996,
        "storage_key": "3cb81a9f-533d-413b-af65-918e2db9fbc2",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "3cb81a9f-533d-413b-af65-918e2db9fbc2",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Cinematic assembly schemas</b> can now reference existing level sequences and cinematic assemblies as templates for the sequencer timeline. You can specify a target template and have that timeline recreated every time you create a new cinematic assembly using the schema.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Design your base timeline in advance.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Reduce repetition and error when creating shots, scenes, lighting sequences, and more!",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Available when using the <b>Cinematic Assembly Tools (CAT)</b> plugin.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Duplicate Assemblies with Reference Replacement",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138756,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Duplicate Assemblies with Reference Replacement",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138756,
        "file_name": "image46.gif",
        "file_size": 391542,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:53:35.399+00:00",
        "height": 275,
        "width": 800,
        "storage_key": "e7058df9-1cad-43f8-8243-f42e3d09b6a2",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "e7058df9-1cad-43f8-8243-f42e3d09b6a2",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can now duplicate assemblies to a new location with reference replacement. You no longer need to manually create, rename, and re-reference subsequences when duplicating an assembly.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Duplicate to a new location and rename automatically based on assembly metadata and tokens.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Subsequences are procedurally duplicated, renamed, and re-referenced to ensure no references are left behind, changing all of your old shots.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Accessible easily from a clean and simple wizard.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "SMPTE 2110&nbsp;—&nbsp;Nvidia Rivermax BlueField-3 Support",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138757,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "SMPTE 2110 Nvidia Rivermax BlueField-3 Support",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138757,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 364033,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T21:54:19.535+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "7274259f-f01b-4948-95db-dd7b8ccb9124",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "7274259f-f01b-4948-95db-dd7b8ccb9124",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The Nvidia Rivermax plugin in Unreal Engine 5.7 supports both BlueField-2 and BlueField-3 DPUs with validated functionality in nDisplay for In-Camera VFX.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "k1Vl",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

```json
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Other Notes",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                },
                {
                  "type": "enhanced_list",
                  "style": "unordered",
                  "items": [
                    [
                      {
                        "type": "paragraph",
                        "content": "UE has been updated to use Gradle 8.7, and the Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) version we use has been updated to version 8.5.2. If you are using Android Studio for debugging, we recommend using Android Studio Koala. Ladybug also works, but does not update Gradle or AGP from the above versions.",
                        "settings": {
                          "is_hidden": false
                        }
                      }
                    ],
                    [
                      {
                        "type": "paragraph",
                        "content": "When you choose your NDK level, you should target android-26, which supports Android 8.0+.",
                        "settings": {
                          "is_hidden": false
                        }
                      }
                    ]
                  ],
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
```
